description: This project is my vertical spinner battlebot, "Untitled Document". I designed this in around 18 hours. It uses a large 2400kv hub motor for the main weapon, and two smaller brushed motors for the main wheels. It uses two large forks at the front to get under other bots, pushing them up into the spinner. I hope to be able to compete with this bot at the GSCRL competition, and I'm very optimistic about its performance. I have always been interested in robotics, and I am the build lead on my highschool robotics team. I have also built a Voron 2.4, along with other smaller printers. I would love to be able to build this bot!
ESP USB - A ESP32 dev board that fits inside your usb port. (Aka my project used to train rf skillz)
My project is a fully 3D-printed drone that can achieve speeds up to 100mph! In the future, I hope to add mounting points to the frame for additions. (Turrets, carrying stuff, etc.)
I made a Macro Pad for my first hardware project and it was great I had a lot of fun learning!!! Looking forward to making a keyboard
i made a hackpad with six buttons that can pause music, skip both directions, turn the volume up and down, and open the calculator
My project is a hackpad designed to game. It has the four necessary keys that can be used as the arrows and to add personalization I extrude some things into it. It also makes it look very good.
STM32 dev board!!! + In a rpi pico format for drop in replacement Has usb support, dual clock for accurate time and adc support. Plus I tried to keep it as cheap as possible
A custom hackpad with 8 switches, an encoder, and an OLED! This is v2 because the first one was riddled with bugs and never saw the light of day.
It's macropad based on the tutorial HackPad. It has 6 keys, 2 LEDs and it uses XIAO RB2040. It runs on KMK firmware.
This is a simple monochromatic LED controller for Home Assistant. It is powered by ESPHome on a XIAO ESP32-C3.
A HackPad for #hackpad, it has 4 keys and a PCB made in KiCad, the case is designed in Onshape!
My proect is a filament recycling system.
An Fast prototyping board for
It is a mecanical Macro pad that is built to be used with AutoHotKey
This is a keyboard for the game Geometry Dash
Designed Macroboard from scratch . Used Esp32 and made macroboard hot swapable
This is a custom hackpad that is basically a standard numberpad. This is my first project that I've tried to make for Hackclub
a macropad with 5 keys and a rotary encoder for common functions (vol up/down, browser back) this is my first hardware project like ever so please excuse all my dumb mistakes 😭

DailyPad is a customizable macropad designed to enhance daily productivity. Built with open-source tools and hardware, it's perfect for developers, makers, and anyone looking to streamline their workflow. Right now it is programmed to take me to the websites I use the most in my daily life.
a macropad for playing the platformer celeste! (the basic keys anyway..)
SkyScan would be autonomous drone for capturing images to use to generate 3D scans. Photogrammetry and 3DGS. Capture abandoned buildings from the sky and keep them standing forever
It's a mini macropad with LEDs that light up when a key is pressed. It runs based of a XIAO RP2040
I have designed a full size keyboard with added roary encoders and a programmable fader. I have the MCU onboard to minimise size.
Wireless split mechanical keyboard with Choc switches and 2 independent halves.

I set out to build a voice assistant that had a small footprint (allowing it to fit easily in any space), A high quality speaker (for voice clarity and music) and a high power processor (to allow for on device speech processing allowing audio to stay on device). My design consists of a cylinder like shape with a visible speaker at the top. There is a status LED strip in a ring at the bottom to show the devices status and give a visual indicator of the device being activated. There are a ring of holes near the base to allow for sound to travel into the microphones. The holes at the bottom also make the design act as a transmission line speaker giving better bass. The design has been specially calculated to have optimal acoustic qualities.
A macropad in the size of a credit card for portability. Also functions as a business card.3

A mechanical WASD type macropad with multitude of customisation options (working on them) that can allow you to use it as a music controller, WASD for video games, volume, brightness controls or just a regular macropad with tons of shortcuts!

The Prince Keeb is a 54-key, low-profile, split keyboard. It uses RJ11 jacks to allow the two halves to communicate with each other, has a thumb cluster for shifting layers, and is ultra-thin to improve typing comfort.

My project is a first-of-its kind delta/quantum delta hybrid 3D printer. Each side needs to move both half the distance and speed to achieve a requested travel move. So, it could be very fast. It is also very small and invertible.

A macropad with 9 keys, 1 EC11 Rotary Encoder, 9 through-hole 1N4148 Diodes, 1 SK6812 MINI, and a XIAO RP2040 as the mcu. Rotary encoder changes volume with basic mapping for keys. Extra features include backlight, slightly visible pcb, silkscreen, and neopixel.
It is a button and a rotary encoder, to optimize photo editing in lightroom (with a backlight inside!)

Chord is a spotify carthing replacement(without the controls). It's powered by a Seeed Studio Xiao ESP32S3 and the display is Seeed Studio's "Round Display for Seeed Studio XIAO". It fetches the song name, artist, and cover via the spotify developer api.

This is my first PCB project! I've got more ideas, but I wanted to knock out this starter project to get an idea of how it all works. It's got nine keys and two rotary encoders wired in a matrix (the space between the encoders is skipped).
My Project is a hackpad built for MIDI drumpads in mind supporting USB C audio output and wireless drumpad capabilities

My project is a cheap, easy to use PCR thermocycler! A PCR thermocycler is a machine that heats up and cools down to specific temperatures to allow the polymerase-chain-reaction to occur. It is one of the most vital tools in biotechnology and biohacking! My specific machine holds one pcr tube, and is controlled by a webpage served to you from an access point generated by the machine.
A keypad that has arrow keys for any web platformer or racing game that I play, with music control buttons above.

My project is making a mushroom lamp, the lamp will be controlled by a arduino and it takes input from a rotary encoder at the front to control the brightness and color of the led's in the lamp, this will make it perfect for a lamp to read by or mood lighting.

My project is a small and compact plug and play USB audio to FM Transmitter. You plug it into your computer and start playing music and this board will automatically transmit it over a set FM frequency. This board is designed to be plug and play requiring no additional software to be installed on your computer. However you will need a microcontroller to program the board if you need to add custom settings like transmitting frequency (default is 86MHz). Programming the board should be pretty easy as there is a PH2.0 connector on board for I2C connection to your microcontroller.

Custom ergo keyboard! I have large fingers and I've been getting a lot of carpal tunnel and other hand issues because of my Apple Magic Keyboard, so I've made a custom one! It's been designed to fit my hands and my needs perfectly, plus is declaratively designed using Ergogen. I also hand routed the entire thing to be capable of reversing.
